summary: Excellent Charlie‑Miller soundboard capture of an upbeat ’78 show with standout The Other One, Scarlet→Fire and vivid Jerry‑Billy banter
review: The SBD transfer by Charlie Miller is crystal clear, delivering vivid stage banter and a punchy mix despite a brief dead‑air gap after “From the Heart of Me” and a slightly sloppy opening cut‑off. Musically the concert shines – long, melodic first‑set jams (Candyman, Sugaree, Stagger Lee) flow into a fiery Scarlet→Fire opener, a 13‑minute mind‑bending The Other One and a tight drum/Wharf Rat segment that many reviewers call the highlight.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers gave 4–5 stars and praised both performance and sound quality; a few low‑star outliers mention minor glitches, yielding an overall strong but not flawless rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Charlie Miller SBD transfer is crisp and loud with vivid banter; a ~45‑second dead‑air after “From the Heart of Me” and a slightly sloppy cut‑off opening are the only noticeable quirks.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set features long, melodic jams (Candyman, Sugaree, Stagger Lee); second set builds with a fiery Scarlet→Fire opener, an expansive The Other One and a tight drum/Wharf Rat segment – overall pacing is strong and engaging.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His guitar work gets special praise (astonishing squeal, “mind‑bending” solos) and his banter with Billy (“Dick‑Lips”, “Cum‑Gums”) is highlighted.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bobby’s slide in the mix is noted as a bit puzzling to some listeners.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s playing shines, especially on Sugaree; this was his last Chicago show and reviewers commend his fill‑in of harmonic holes.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey (and Bill) are mentioned for their tight interplay; the Ollin Arageed drum segment is singled out as a highlight.
